meta.libraryPositioning: Arrow template library for directional relationships, process chains, chevrons, branches, cycles, architecture-layer lift arrows, connector routes, and PPT-style structure arrows. Use alongside charts_index.json when a page's main visual grammar is arrow-based.

connector_arrow_set.summary: Pick for thin straight, vertical, bidirectional, and orthogonal connector arrows between existing nodes. Skip for wide phase chains (use block_chevron_sequence).

block_chevron_sequence.summary: Pick for 3-6 horizontal phase chains where each stage is a chunky chevron block with editable labels. Skip for plain node-to-node workflow (use connector_arrow_set or process_flow).

block_long_gradient_arrow.summary: Pick for a single dominant strategy path, growth direction, or conclusion pointer with room for a headline inside the arrow body. Skip for multi-stage processes (use block_chevron_sequence).

split_branch_arrows.summary: Pick for one source splitting into 2-3 downstream paths, or multiple streams converging into one target. Skip for hub diagrams with many symmetric spokes (use hub_spoke or hub_inward_arrows).

cycle_feedback_arrows.summary: Pick for 4-step feedback loops, PDCA-style cycles, or iterative closed-loop governance. Skip for linear progressions (use block_chevron_sequence).

data_flow_swimlanes.summary: Pick for cross-lane data movement, platform handoffs, or system integration flows where each arrow should be labeled. Skip for business process nodes without lanes (use process_flow).

dashed_bus_up_taps.summary: Pick for a horizontal dotted bus line with multiple upward tap arrows connecting a lower capability row to upper cards, like architecture support relationships. Skip for solid process chains (use block_chevron_sequence).

bottom_lift_arrow_row.summary: Pick for a bottom baseline sending 3-6 thick upward arrows into the layer above, like foundation capability lift or infrastructure support. Skip for thin dependency lines (use connector_arrow_set).

architecture_layer_connectors.summary: Pick for stacked architecture diagrams where horizontal divider bands need upward and downward connectors between rows. Skip for freeform workflow pages without layers (use data_flow_swimlanes).

dashed_orthogonal_dependency_bus.summary: Pick for dotted orthogonal dependency lines with elbows, vertical taps, and arrowheads across multiple cards. Skip for simple straight arrows (use connector_arrow_set).

solid_up_arrow_set.summary: Pick for reusable filled upward arrows with small, medium, and large weights for foundation-to-capability emphasis. Skip for long horizontal strategy direction (use block_long_gradient_arrow).

multi_level_support_lift.summary: Pick for bottom foundation bands supporting middle systems and upper services through repeated upward arrows and a central broad lift. Skip for only thin dependency taps (use dashed_bus_up_taps).

ribbon_chevron_bus.summary: Pick for a thin horizontal ribbon of small chevrons showing light progression across many cards. Skip for chunky phase blocks with labels (use block_chevron_sequence).

converge_to_platform_arrow.summary: Pick for 3-5 streams converging into one central platform or governance block. Skip for one source splitting outward (use split_branch_arrows).

distribute_from_platform_arrow.summary: Pick for one platform or foundation block distributing capability upward or outward into 3-5 services. Skip for many sources converging inward (use converge_to_platform_arrow).

vertical_feedback_ladder.summary: Pick for vertical up/down feedback between two stacked layers, with separate ascent and return arrows. Skip for closed circular loops (use cycle_feedback_arrows).

side_rail_arrow_frame.summary: Pick for architecture pages needing left or right side rail arrows that bracket multiple rows and indicate end-to-end coverage. Skip for internal card-to-card links (use connector_arrow_set).

pptpack_gradient_long_right_arrow.summary: Pick for a clean long rightward PPT arrow indicating overall direction, migration, or target-state movement. Skip for larger perspective main-chain arrows (use pptpack4_gradient_mainchain_01).

pptpack_gradient_short_right_arrow.summary: Pick for a compact rightward arrow between two blocks when the arrow should be visible but not dominate. Skip for full-width strategy paths (use pptpack_gradient_long_right_arrow).

pptpack_gradient_double_head_horizontal_.summary: Pick for bidirectional exchange, mutual influence, two-way data flow, or comparison between two sides. Skip for center-node bidirectional flow (use pptpack3_gradient_center_node_bidirectional_flow).

pptpack_gradient_process_chevron_3_steps.summary: Pick for a three-step process chain with PPT-style gradient chevrons. Skip for four-stage processes (use pptpack_gradient_process_chevron_4_steps).

pptpack_gradient_process_chevron_4_steps.summary: Pick for a four-step process chain with clean connected chevrons. Skip for a single long direction arrow (use pptpack_gradient_long_right_arrow).

pptpack_gradient_chevron_ribbon_arrow.summary: Pick for a ribbon-like chevron arrow showing continuous progress or a streamlined path. Skip for separate labeled process steps (use pptpack_gradient_process_chevron_3_steps).

pptpack_gradient_up_arrow.summary: Pick for a clean upward PPT arrow indicating lift, growth, or promotion without 3D styling. Skip for broad upward lift (use pptpack3_gradient_wide_up_arrow).

pptpack_gradient_down_arrow.summary: Pick for a clean downward PPT arrow indicating deployment, drill-down, or top-down delivery. Skip for lane-based data flows (use data_flow_swimlanes).

pptpack_gradient_converging_to_right_arr.summary: Pick for two inputs converging into a rightward outcome, platform, or target. Skip for many streams into one platform (use converge_to_platform_arrow).

pptpack_gradient_diverging_fork_arrow.summary: Pick for one path splitting into two rightward branches or differentiated choices. Skip for three or more downstream paths (use split_branch_arrows).

pptpack_gradient_center_up_main_path_arr.summary: Pick for a horizontal baseline with a central upward lift, useful for main-path uplift or capability emergence. Skip for repeated foundation supports (use bottom_lift_arrow_row).

pptpack_gradient_low_profile_roof_arrow.summary: Pick for a low-profile bridge or roof arrow spanning multiple blocks in architecture diagrams. Skip for perspective main-chain emphasis (use pptpack4_gradient_mainchain_01).

pptpack_gradient_arch_bridge_flow_arrow.summary: Pick for a subtle arch bridge flow over a foundation or cross-layer connection. Skip for platform-up lift arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_platform_up_arrow).

pptpack_gradient_horizontal_to_down_arro.summary: Pick for rightward flow turning downward into delivery, implementation, or landing. Skip for pure downward emphasis (use pptpack_gradient_down_arrow).

pptpack_gradient_horizontal_to_up_arrow.summary: Pick for rightward flow turning upward into escalation, uplift, or target capability. Skip for pure upward growth (use pptpack_gradient_up_arrow).
